corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: yolo model processes images in real-time at 45 frames per second a smaller version of the network fast yolo processes an astounding 155 frames per second while still achieving double the map of other real-time detectors compared to state-of-the-art detection systems yolo makes more localization errors but is less likely to predict false positives on background finally yolo learns very general representations of objects it outperforms other detection methods including dpm and r-cnn when generalizing from natural images to other

top_candidates.sentence: We also present VOC 2012 results and compare mAP to current state-of-the-art methods.
To further examine the differences between YOLO and state-of-the-art detectors, we look at a detailed breakdown of results on VOC 2007.
This is lower than the current state of the art, closer to the original R-CNN using VGG-16, see Table .
However, on other categories like cat and train YOLO achieves higher performance.
Compared to state-of-the-art detection systems, YOLO makes more localization errors but is less likely to predict false positives on background.
It outperforms other detection methods, including DPM and R-CNN, when generalizing from natural images to other domains like artwork.
Using our system, you only look once (YOLO) at an image to predict what objects are present and where they are.
Furthermore, YOLO achieves more than twice the mean average precision of other real-time systems.
Furthermore, YOLO achieves more than twice the mean average precision of other real-time systems.
When trained on natural images and tested on artwork, YOLO outperforms top detection methods like DPM and R-CNN by a wide margin.
When trained on natural images and tested on artwork, YOLO outperforms top detection methods like DPM and R-CNN by a wide margin.
When trained on natural images and tested on artwork, YOLO outperforms top detection methods like DPM and R-CNN by a wide margin.
We also present VOC 2012 results and compare mAP to current state-of-the-art methods.
Our model also uses relatively coarse features for predicting bounding boxes since our architecture has multiple downsampling layers from the input image.
Unlike classifier-based approaches, YOLO is trained on a loss function that directly corresponds to detection performance and the entire model is trained jointly.
Fast YOLO is the fastest general-purpose object detector in the literature and YOLO pushes the state-of-the-art in real-time object detection.
We present YOLO, a new approach to object detection.
Our unified architecture is extremely fast.
YOLO still lags behind state-of-the-art detection systems in accuracy.
YOLO still lags behind state-of-the-art detection systems in accuracy.
We introduce YOLO, a unified model for object detection.

downstream_contract.intended_use: Feed this JSON to a later local open-source model to write an overview.
downstream_contract.grounding_rule: The downstream generator should write from evidence_units.location.sentence and context_window, not from unsupported outside knowledge.
downstream_contract.score_warning: Scores are ranking and filtering signals, not calibrated probabilities.
